claim: Obsidian Web Clipper is the official, free, open-source browser extension that saves articles, highlights, and selections as portable Markdown files with template-driven metadata extraction across Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge, Brave, Arc, Orion, and Vivaldi.
quote: Highlight and capture the web in your favorite browser... All content stored locally in your Obsidian vault. Clips saved as Markdown files you control.

claim: Reviewers in 2026 frame the Obsidian Web Clipper as a free alternative to Readwise that wins on deep vault integration and template customization, while Readwise Reader still wins for heavy in-line annotation flows.
quote: If you are building a long-term knowledge library and want deep Obsidian integration with rich template customization, the native Obsidian Web Clipper is the right foundation. If you annotate extensively as you read, Readwise Reader adds a layer that the native clipper cannot match.

claim: The official Readwise Obsidian plugin auto-syncs highlights from Kindle, Apple Books, Instapaper, Pocket, Twitter, PDFs, etc. into per-source notes, append-only so nothing in the vault is overwritten, with Jinja2 templates for metadata.
quote: If the highlight is from a new book, article, or Twitter account, a new page in the Obsidian vault will be made; if the highlight is from an existing source, the highlight will be appended to the bottom of the existing page.

claim: The community Obsidian Hypothesis plugin syncs web article highlights and annotations from Hypothes.is into the vault on startup or manual trigger, updating existing articles and supporting Nunjucks templates.
quote: An Obsidian.md plugin that syncs highlights from Hypothesis... sync web article highlights/annotations on Obsidian startup or manual trigger, update existing articles with new highlights and annotations.

claim: A widely shared Mac/iOS pattern combines Advanced URI, Templater, and Hotkeys for Templates so a global hotkey or iOS Shortcut creates a dated note, then Opt+A/D/M archive, trash, or move it during inbox triage; the next note auto-opens.
quote: creates a new dated note with contents entered on the go... automatically opens the next note in the folder after the previous note has been processed.

claim: QuickAdd is the dominant capture-template plugin in 2026 — a single command appends a timestamped bullet to today's daily note (creating it if missing), with macros chaining template + capture + script for richer flows.
quote: Capture allows you to quickly add content to predefined files... Daily Notes/{{DATE:gggg-MM-DD}}.md... appends a timestamped bullet to today's note.

claim: The Inbox Organiser plugin captures every new note into an inbox folder and periodically prompts the user to file each into a target folder, encoding the 'capture first, organize later' principle as a recurring nag.
quote: Obsidian plugin to capture any new notes into an inbox and periodically prompt to organise these into other folders within the vault.

claim: Community PKM consensus is that an Inbox folder is the single point of entry for mobile/quick captures and unprocessed notes, with QuickAdd + Templater applying a quick-note template at capture time and a weekly review pass for triage.
quote: The Inbox folder is one of the most important folders... where temp notes, quick notes, or notes captured on mobile are stored.

claim: Mobile capture is the most common pain point on r/ObsidianMD; Obsidian has mobile quick capture on its official roadmap but most users currently rely on third-party companion apps (Quick Draft, Fleeting Notes) or an Android home-screen widget that writes to daily notes.
quote: Mobile is where Obsidian gets its most consistent criticism on Reddit, with iOS and Android apps working but feeling less polished than the desktop version.

claim: PDF++ turns Obsidian into an Obsidian-native PDF annotator: highlights are stored as Markdown backlinks (not embedded in the PDF), color-coded with `&color=NAME`, surfaced in the backlinks pane, and survive even if the plugin disappears.
quote: you can annotate PDF files with highlights just by linking to text selection... The value of the markdown format does not lie in the file extension!

claim: Paper Clipper adds a custom URI scheme for importing academic PDFs into the vault with structured metadata, complementing PDF++ for a Zotero-lite paper capture workflow inside Obsidian.
quote: Paper Clipper - Obsidian Plugins that Capture and manage academic papers.

claim: The Obsidian Terminal community plugin (xterm.js-based) embeds shells inside the editor, captures ANSI-colored output, exports full sessions as Markdown files, and supports regex find-in-terminal for command-output triage.
quote: exporting complete terminal sessions as markdown files, with automatic conversion of ANSI color codes... regex-capable searches through thousands of lines of output instantly.

claim: The Console Markdown plugin renders fenced `console` code blocks as styled command + output blocks, giving a low-friction way to paste shell commands and their results into a note without a live terminal.
quote: An Obsidian plugin which renders console commands and their output.

claim: SnippetBase indexes every fenced code block across the vault, supports filter-by-folder/language, free-text search, favorites, and copy-to-clipboard — converting daily notes into a searchable code snippet library.
quote: indexes fenced code snippets within a vault... filter by folder and language... search with free text... Mark snippets as favorites for quick access.

claim: Logstravaganza proxies `console.*()` calls and writes log output plus uncaught exceptions and promise rejections to a vault file in NDJSON, Markdown table, or code-block format — the standard recipe for capturing error logs from Obsidian itself.
quote: intercepts and writes all `console.*()` output to a file... captures uncaught exceptions, including those in asynchronous code and promises.

claim: A common screenshot-capture pattern uses the OS shortcut (Cmd+Ctrl+Shift+4 on Mac, Win+Shift+S on Windows) to grab the image, paste it into a Fleeting Notes browser-extension note with annotations underneath, and auto-sync into Obsidian.
quote: Use Command + Control + Shift + 4 (Mac) or Windows + Shift + S (Windows)... Click the Fleeting Notes button and add a title... Paste the captured image into the note... Add any annotations you want at the bottom of the note.

claim: The Copy Image plugin closes the loop in the other direction: right-click any image already in a note to copy it back to the system clipboard, useful for re-pasting screenshots into tickets or chats.
quote: Easily copy image to clipboard by right clicking image.

claim: Forum and r/PKMS community consensus emphasizes 'capture first, organize later' — trying to categorize ideas at capture time kills creativity, so the daily note acts as universal inbox and triage happens during a scheduled (often weekly) review.
quote: capturing first and organizing later... trying to categorize an idea the moment it strikes kills creativity.

claim: The 2026 Obsidian Web Clipper update added YouTube transcript capture and an improved Reader Mode that strips boilerplate; r/ObsidianMD users build templates auto-injecting video length, channel, and publication date into clipped notes.
quote: create templates that automatically add metadata—video length, channel name, publication date—when clipping transcripts.

claim: Reviewers in 2026 recommend pairing the Web Clipper with downstream plugins (Dataview, Linter, Auto Note Mover) so clipped notes land in an inbox folder, get cleaned up, and surface in dashboards rather than rotting unread.
quote: 7 Obsidian Plugins That Make Web Clipping Actually Useful (2026).
